focus: rank proposals plus cost-latency analysis and executive-summary pre-work

summary: Iteration 7 ranked the remediation set by accuracy-gain x simplicity x reversibility. The best-first ordering is advisor command-surface normalization, Gate 3 deep-loop positive markers, Gate 3 broader resume markers, Gate 3 mixed-tail write exception, then threshold tuning.

The current Gate 3 vocabulary has 30 entries. Estimated trigger growth is +8 for the deep-loop proposal, +3 for broader resume/context markers, and +11 for the full staged bundle. All runtime costs remain negligible because the classifier is still a short linear scan over one prompt string.

Command-surface normalization needs an explicit-invocation guard. 13 of the 14 command-surface false positives are true command-invocation prompts whose gold label is the owning skill, but one row (rr-iter3-070) only mentions /spec_kit:resume as a quoted string inside a coding task.
The rolling newInfoRatio values are 0.30, 0.22, and 0.14 after iteration 7, so the research loop has not converged and should not write research.md yet.

The cheapest high-yield fix is to normalize four over-firing command bridges back to their owning skills after scoring, with a guard that suppresses normalization when the command token is merely quoted inside a coding request. This raises advisor exact-match accuracy from 53.5% to 60.0% in the dry run.

The best additive Gate 3 fix is to add explicit deep-loop markers for the /spec_kit deep-research and deep-review surfaces plus loop-wording variants. That patch removes the largest no_match cluster and lifts Gate 3 F1 from 68.6% to 83.3% in the dry run.

A narrow write-tail exception still looks worthwhile for mixed prompts, but it should be sequenced after the additive patches because it mostly converts double failures into advisor-only misses instead of directly increasing fully-correct joint outcomes.
